featured.title: European Commission confirms data breach after Europa.eu hack
featured.summary: The European Commission has confirmed a data breach after its Europa.eu web platform was hacked in a cyberattack claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion gang. [...]
featured.reason: Major data breach with potential widespread impact
featured.suggestedTitle: European Commission Data Breach: ShinyHunters Attack Confirmed
featured.suggestedSummary: The European Commission has confirmed a data breach following a cyberattack by the ShinyHunters group on its Europa.eu platform. The breach involved unauthorized access to sensitive data, potentially affecting multiple systems and users. ShinyHunters, known for targeting high-profile organizations, claimed responsibility. Security teams should assess their exposure to similar threats and enhance monitoring of external-facing systems to mitigate risks.

featured.title: Critical Fortinet Forticlient EMS flaw now exploited in attacks
featured.summary: Attackers are now actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in Fortinet's FortiClient EMS platform, according to threat intelligence company Defused. [...]
featured.suggestedTitle: Fortinet FortiClient EMS Flaw Exploited: Immediate Patch Required
featured.suggestedSummary: A critical vulnerability in Fortinet's FortiClient EMS platform, identified as CVE-2026-21643, is under active exploitation. This flaw allows attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely, posing a severe risk to organizations using vulnerable versions. The exploitation of this vulnerability emphasizes the need for immediate patching and review of security configurations to prevent potential breaches.

featured.title: Critical Citrix NetScaler memory flaw actively exploited in attacks
featured.summary: Hackers are exploiting a critical severity vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-3055, in Citrix  NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway appliances to obtain sensitive data. [...]
featured.reason: Critical Citrix vulnerability with active exploitation
featured.suggestedTitle: Citrix NetScaler CVE-2026-3055 Exploited: Patch Now
featured.suggestedSummary: Hackers are exploiting a critical vulnerability in Citrix NetScaler, tracked as CVE-2026-3055, which allows for unauthorized remote code execution. This vulnerability affects multiple versions of Citrix NetScaler, necessitating immediate patch application. Security teams should prioritize updating affected systems and monitoring for unusual activity to prevent exploitation.

quickNews.title: Healthcare tech firm CareCloud says hackers stole patient data
quickNews.summary: Healthcare IT firm CareCloud has disclosed a data breach incident that exposed sensitive data and caused a network disruption lasting approximately eight hours. [...]

quickNews.title: New RoadK1ll WebSocket implant used to pivot on breached networks
quickNews.summary: A newly identified malicious implant named RoadK1ll is enabling threat actors to quietly move from a compromised host to other systems on the network. [...]

quickNews.title: AI-Powered 'DeepLoad' Malware Steals Credentials, Evades Detection
quickNews.summary: The massive amount of junk code that hides the malware's logic from security scans was almost certainly generated by AI, researchers say.

quickNews.title: OpenAI Patches ChatGPT Data Exfiltration Flaw and Codex GitHub Token Vulnerability
quickNews.summary:
A previously unknown vulnerability in OpenAI ChatGPT allowed sensitive conversation data to be exfiltrated without user knowledge or consent, according to new findings from Check Point.
"A single malicious prompt could turn an otherwise ordinary conversation into a covert exfiltration channel, leaking user messages, uploaded files, and other sensitive content," the cybersecurity company said in

quickNews.title: F5 BIG-IP Vulnerability Reclassified as RCE, Under Exploitation
quickNews.summary: CVE-2025-53521 was initially disclosed in October as a high-severity denial-of-service (DoS) flaw, but new information has revealed the bug is actually much more dangerous.
